title: viola bellidifolia subsp. typica
intro: Viola bellidifolia subsp. typica, also known as the pale pansy or small field pansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family, Violaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Viola bellidifolia subsp. typica, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to support overall health and well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits.
